enGB: This is a test feat, how very jank.

Acrobats are people who learned that the easiest way through life is rarely the most direct, and that flexibility and quick thinking are the most important things to stay alive in dire situations. While they generally prefer to rely on their skills and natural charisma to avoid confrontation when possible, acrobats are capable fighters. The acrobats signature fighting style tends to favor disrupting their opponents rather than simply overpowering them, which reflects their intricate lifestyles and distaste for directness.

Acrobats are masters of combat maneuvers and excel at applying different conditions on enemies. They are lightly armored but are able to easily position themselves to avoid being targeted and are agile enough to avoid some hits coming their way.

An acrobat is proficient with all simple weapons plus the flail, heavy flail, two-bladed sword and nuchaku. When using one of these extra weapons or a quarterstaff, the acrobat can treat them as light weapons for the purpose of using Weapon Finesse. They are proficient with light armor, but not with shields.

At 2nd level, and every 4th level afterwards, an acrobat gains a bonus feat in addition to those gained from normal advancement. These bonus feats must be selected from those listed as Combat Maneuver Feats. The acrobat can select a bonus feat even if they do not meet its attribute or feat requirements.
At 1st level, an acrobat gets a +1 bonus to their CMB and CMD. That bonus increases by 1 at 5th level and every 4th level afterwards.
At 1st level and every 8th level afterward, an acrobat chooses a maneuver type to become their specialty. When performing a specialty maneuver, the acrobat's Maneuver Mastery bonus to CMB is doubled.

At 2nd level and every 3rd level afterwards, an acrobat gains one opportunistic manipulation.  An opportunistic manipulation can either be used as a standard action or be set as the acrobat's active manipulation. Whenever the acrobat misses a melee attack, they will perform their active manipulation against the attack's target. An acrobat can only perform one opportunistic manipulation per turn, further misses will have no effect.  Some opportunistic manipulations allow their target to make a saving throw, the DC of these saves is equal to 10 + half the acrobat's level + the acrobat's Charisma modifier.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you try to push your opponent back.  You perform a bull rush combat maneuver against your target.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you try to hamper your opponent.  You perform a dirty trick combat maneuver against your target.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you try to sicken your opponent.  You perform a sickening dirty trick combat maneuver against your target.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you try to blind your opponent.  You perform a blinding dirty trick combat maneuver against your target.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you try to entangle your opponent.  You perform an entangling dirty trick combat maneuver against your target.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you try to disarm your opponent.  You perform a disarm combat maneuver against your target.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you try to peel off your opponents armor.  You perform a sunder armor maneuver against your target.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you try to make your opponent fall.  You perform a trip combat maneuver against your target.

This feat gives the character an ability to perform the grapple combat maneuver.  It also grants a +2 bonus to CMB when performing this maneuver and a +2 bonus to CMD when defending against it.  You can attempt to grapple a foe in melee as a standard action. If your combat maneuver is successful, both the target and you are grappled.  Grappled characters cannot move, take a -2 penalty on all attack rolls and a-4 penalty to Dexterity-based checks, cannot make attacks of opportunity, and whenever they cast a spell or use a spell-like ability, they must make a concentration check against a DC of 10 + the spell's level + the grappler's CM or lose the spell.  The target of the grapple attempts to escape every round by making a successful Athletics or Mobility skill check, or Strength check. The DC of this check is the grappler's CMD.  While grappling the target, the grappler cannot take standard actions.  Each round, the grappler makes a grapple maneuver check with a +5 circumstance bonus. On success, the target is pinned and takes damage equal to that of the grappler's attack with the highest damage dice. If the check fails this ends the grapple for both the grappler and the target.  As a free action, the grappler can end the grapple for both themselves and the target.   Pinned characters suffer the following penalties: Character suffers all of the penalties from being grappled. Character loses their Dexterity and dodge bonuses to AC. Character cannot take standard actions. Character suffers a -4 penalty to AC.
Release your opponent and end both of your grappled states.
This creature is grappled, taking a -2 to its attack rolls and a -4 to its Dexterity.

You receive a +2 bonus on checks made to attempt or maintain a grapple.  This bonus stacks with the bonus granted by Grapple.   While you are grappling someone, you can take attack for free at the beginning of each round.
You are wailing on your grapple opponent, getting a free attack each round.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you try to catch your opponent.  You perform a grapple combat maneuver against your target.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you whisper threats of violence in your opponent's ear.  Your opponent becomes shaken for 1 round per 2 levels (Will negate).
Use Spook as a Standard Action.

Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you tell your opponent of the horrors you're going to make them suffer through.  Your opponent becomes frightened for 1 round per 4 levels (Will negate).
Use Terrify as a Standard Action.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you spin your opponent around, making them loose focus and balance.  Your target becomes confused for 1 round per 5 levels (Will negates).
Use Befuddle as a Standard Action.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you hit your opponent in the head.  Your target becomes stunned for 1 round per 5 levels (Fortitude negates).
Use Astonish as a Standard Action.
Using the distraction created by your failed attack, you quickly block your opponent's pressure points.  Your target becomes paralysed for 1 round per 9 levels (Fortitude negates).
Use Stupefy as a Standard Action.
